Okay, we're going to get ready to start again. Now, some people have earlier flights to catch than others. And in the interest of covering everything that I can before those people have to leave, I'm going to ask this follow-up, the following question. What is it that you may have come here to learn that I haven't covered yet that you want me to... Now, I want you to remember something. You're also...and you're not going to get them at any financial expense. So, if there's something you don't remember, that will be in the audio tapes, video tapes and the transcripts. In addition to that, you're also going to get a letter that goes over everything that happened in this seminar and everything in my sales letter, because once in a while, I will not cover a certain point, like the mechanical steps of rolling out a mailing to summarize that. So, what I want to make sure though is that if there's any fundamental things, I want to do them before the lunch break, because some people won't be able to come back. So, does anybody have a fundamental thing? I don't know. The mailing piece is similar in construction to try to get a customer back. Yeah, that's a good question. When you want a customer back, you should look at it as a brand new sales effort, and you should go full bore just the way you did to get the original customer. I'm someone who fails to do that with my own customers more than I should, and I think not only it can be the same format, but more importantly, it should be a long copy, very appreciative message telling them, reminding them of the benefits they've enjoyed in the past and telling them what they're going to get in the future, and preferably offering premiums for coming on board again. And that's something you should really work at. What else? Yeah. Yes, I will. What you addressed was number of pieces of the mailing, and how they should be ordered, what should be in a separate line from all of them. The sequence? Okay, yes, I'll do that. Let me write these things down. One is about how to get clients, right? I'll get you all the clients you want. This one, I don't think in your sales letter. That's all right. I will tell you though, if you haven't already invested a lot of time and energy learning how to do those, I wouldn't because a simple letter will often beat them. Where the Magilogues really work is where people are mailing hundreds of millions of pieces a year, and they need a dramatic change in format because their prospects get so much mail from them. Monday, people are making, is a transition day from the weekend to work. Friday is, I can't wait till five o'clock. It's a transition day. Weekends, people want to be off. So you can't control that completely. That is not what you're mailing, the success of your mailing will rise or fall on. But it's one of those things that if you can control it, you should. In the longer streams when you start getting the bottom feeders, yes, but right now, no, I'm sorry. And you just need to get their attention more and more. You need to use different format mailings, but you don't need those people to call for free recorded messages. Free recorded messages is sort of a funnel technique. You don't need a funnel. Those people are already at the second end of the funnel. You see what I'm saying? Okay. It'll be yellow. So I just think it's a better way. And I don't think the reduction in price is significant enough, you know, as compared to the impact. I have not tried it and don't know anything about it. Two letter postcards or double postcard. I don't know. I don't have a thought about that. What do you think about colored papers? You always do everything on white. No, I don't. At least I've never seen anything like this. You know, one of the things that you got to understand that I do when I'm teaching people, I'm generally not talking to people I've worked with for a long time like Sam Fishbind and John Carlson and stuff. So instead of teaching every little detail of how you could alter this, I try and teach what would be one very excellent way to do things. And if I allow novices, and you're not a novice, but if I allow some of these people to get focused on that, they will start worrying more about are they using the right color paper, then they'll be worried about getting the main headline down. That's very important that you don't do that. And, you know, it's pretty tough to go wrong with black ink on white paper. Okay, these things that I have written down in the questions here, I want to talk about. One is the someone that's the same question, the legal problems and male frauds and so forth. Now, I'm going to talk to you like a Dutch uncle about this and tell you the reality of it. The reality of it is that there is no way you can be in business in America today without being technically a criminal. I guarantee you that everybody in this room is breaking the law in the conduct of your daily lives and business, federal, state, local and county laws. There is no way that you can not do that. And there is no way that you can completely bulletproof yourself from this. I want to give you some examples of how true that is. I did business in Key West for a while under the name Key West College of Millionaires. I thought I had ran a file of every alphabet agency there is, but I had not yet run a file of the Department of Education, the Florida Department of Education, who informed me that it was illegal in Florida to use the word college in the name of any business unless you were offering accredited, you could be accredited. You could be accredited without accreditation, whatever, you know, accredited things. And they wanted to be accredited and they were willing to make me accredited. All I had to do was go through all their stuff and do it, and I didn't want to. And so they said, well, you can't use the word college. And I said, well, let me ask you this. I went further on to explain to me that anybody in Florida who teaches anybody anything on how to make more money in their career or their job falls under their jurisdiction. And I said, no, wait a minute. For example, I shoot, I'm pretty good pool player. If I teach people how to make money hustling pool, do I need to be accredited? They said, yes, believe it or not, you do. I said, that is a violation of the First Amendment rights and said, you know, she said, we agree with you. We know that we do it anyway. No more Key West College of Millionaires. It wasn't worth it to struggle with Mike Royco. The national columnist tells the following story. A little old lady lived alone and needed some repairs on her home and advertised free rent in exchange for a handyman who would do repairs around her work. One of these watchdog agencies that keeps us all safe from the villains of society called her and threatened to sue her unless she paid them $900. The base of the suit was sexual discrimination because she said handyman instead of handy person. She scoffed at this nonsensical idea, refused to pay the $900 and found herself saddled with $3,000 in legal bills. Seriously. There's a man whose name I won't use was operating in Miami and was one of the largest mailers in the United States being one of the largest mailers in the United States would be mailing like sometimes more than 2 million letters a week. Nice Scott had a big building. A lot of really neat equipment had employed all kinds of people was an asset to the community. However, he committed the single most committed crime in the eyes of the federal authorities. He had become a success and people who become federal investigators despise people who become a success. They know that there is no way that that can be done legally and ethically because they themselves cannot do it. And so it came to pass that some years back there were new laws enacted to help the feds get drug dealers. And it's the forfeiture and seizure laws. Now here's the way those laws work. You're a drug dealer or they suspect you're a drug dealer. They go to a judge and they say, we think Jerry Buckwheat is a drug dealer. We're not ready to take him to court yet, but we want to freeze all of his assets, you know, in case he tries to abscond with them and they take the drug dealers house and all his money and his car and yada, yada, yada, yada. Also, they may have witnesses who are willing to testify against the drug dealer and those people are sealed from are protected from being to identify themselves from having to identify themselves. Well, fortunately for all of us, they have now made this law applicable to everybody. You don't have to be a drug dealer. And here's the way it works. This is great. Here's what they did to my friend in Miami. They went to a judge and we said, this guy mails a lot of mail and we think he might be doing something wrong, but we're not prepared to make a case against him. But in the meantime, we want you to confiscate all of his assets allow us to do it. The judge said, why not? So they took his home, his car and freeze one and a half million dollars in his bank account, etc, etc, etc. Now, the first thing he does is he calls up and says, what did I do wrong? And you know what? These there is now understand something. There's been no due process. There has been no indictment, no testimony, no nothing. And so they call and all of this is sealed and he can't respond to what he did wrong because they won't even tell it. Well, who said I did something wrong? They won't tell him that either. They just do it. And you know how often that happens every day. You know, they, if you watch it on 60 minutes, people get off the Dallas airport. The DEA makes a mistake. They think that's guys drug dealer. They take everything he has and he fights it for five years. The punishment is in the process. So what do you do about that? The answer is you have first answer is you have to make a decision. Do you have what it takes to be an entrepreneur in America or do you not? And it is no shame to not have it. But I'll tell you what you don't get is you don't get a guarantee. Now, I do not blame people at all for not wanting to be an entrepreneur and work in safety for the US government who never lays anybody off or IBM who never lays anybody off and all these other industries that are going down the tubes. But if you choose, then you have to go into it realistically. And the first thing you learn is that you might want to write this down. Innocence is no defense. It's just not. It's just not necessarily a defense. So here are some things that you might think about. First of all, always tell the truth. You can you can exaggerate. You can hype it. You can use hyperbole. You can be clever. You can showcase it. You can make put a lot of showmanship in it, but tell the truth. That's one thing. Secondly, fill your orders as quickly as you can. Thirdly, respond to all questions as quickly as you can. Fourthly, make all legitimate refunds as promptly as you can. Fifth, get yourself an attorney. You should never talk to an employee of the federal government in any kind of investigatory capacity. Metrex is being investigated by the FTC. And I got a note from an attorney about two weeks ago for the FTC and said that we are investigating the Metrex Corporation and we would like to talk with you, blah, blah, blah, blah, blah. The reply from my attorney was this, your request for an interview is denied. If you have any further questions, please contact this office. You might want to write that down in case your attorney has trouble with it. Your request for an interview is denied. If you have any further questions, please contact this office. They did contact that office and they assured her that I was not in any way the object of the investigation. I don't give a shit. You know, all the, there is no upside to me talking to these people. Now, if they want me to talk with them, I will and you should too under the following conditions. They subpoena you and they give you immunity. Otherwise, you don't want to talk to them because you know what the Fifth Amendment says because it may tend to incriminate me, believe me, when it comes to the government, hello may tend to incriminate you. Hi, my name is Gary. You're guilty. You know, no, you do not talk to them. Here's the other thing. Last thing I will say about that. The government and other people are like muggers in Central Park. A mugger is an opportunist. He is not trying to win a gold medal for bravery. Can you imagine two muggers walking in Central Park needing some crack cocaine? One guy saying, man, look at that big guy over there. Looks like he just got out of the joint. I bet he's been pumping iron for 10 years. Think we can take him. I don't know, but let's give it a shot. No, no, says no, no, look, look at that old lady over there. She can't hardly walk with the fat person. They go get her, right? It is a curiosity to me by any successful businessman in America would have visible, sizable assets. Tell you something curious about that. I don't think you should use an offshore tax haven to avoid taxes. Taxes aren't the problem. I think maybe you should pay your taxes and put your money in a bank account in the Cayman Islands. And the thing about it, that's not to avoid taxes. That's just to keep it there so that they can't reach and get it. You know, if you're really hungry and there's, you know, there's food on the table, but there's also recipes and ingredients where you can cook it up and make it. If you're really hungry, you're just going to grab what's easy, right? And so at least don't do anything illegal. Pay your taxes, put the money offshore and don't make it easy for them. Tell you something interesting about the Cayman Islands. Their secrecy laws make Switzerland look like blabbermouths. I personally in the past have owned three corporations. And let me tell you how it works in the Cayman Islands. Someone gets off the plane and walks into a bank or any place and says, I'd like to know if John Jones has a checking account here. Here's the reply. Sir, under Cayman law, it is illegal even to ask a question about anything to do with someone else's finances. You have just broken that law. We are calling a constable. He is escorting you to the airport or to the local jail. You can have your choice. If you go to the airport, you will take the first plane out no matter where it's going. Honestly, it is. Is that great? It is illegal even to ask. So those are some things you should think about. Britain. That is so. That is so. What? They do develop information. There's a thing. I had Miami and actually they'll divulge information on drug deals. That could be in fact, curiously enough, they volunteered that and had trouble getting the US Congress to go along with it. They won't divulge information. You know, just just on a plane business transaction or something. Neither will Switzerland. And also you need to know what we're talking about is certainly not making something flawless. We're just talking about making giving you better odds. I mean, if you were filing the lawsuit against me, would you rather I had my money in the First National Bank of Miami Beach or XYZ invisible bank in Canaan? Just think about it. As far as the technicalities are concerned, what you need is an attorney to go there and talk with another attorney that's already there. Instead of doing yourself, there's no big technicalities to it. All right. The sequence. Someone wanted to know about sequence and they wanted to know about the flow of the sales letter. And those two things are connected. So I'll talk about them as one question. Most of you or at least some of you know the ADA acronym, A-I-D-A. In case you don't. A stands for attention. I stands for interest. D stands for desire. And the last A stands for action. And that is the sequence that you want the people to go through when you are writing to them. You want to capture their attention. Then get them interested in what it is that you have to say. Which, which transitions into them making them want it. And then when you've got them as hot for it as you can get it, you go, that's when you make the call to action and go for the clothes. Here's how I like to do the sequence of letters and it's not necessarily perfect. But it's just the way I like to do it. Usually I use a closed faced envelope. And I'm not sure, but I think that most people when they open an envelope, open it from the back like this. And so what I like is the top third of the letter to have a headline. So the letter is the first is next to the back like this. All right. And then behind that is the reply envelope. And then we behind that is the order coupon. Now, the way I like to do lift letters, which are letters that are enclosed that lift the response is on the back of the order coupon. Generally, I make the order coupons. Yellow. And that sets it apart a little bit from the main letter. The Now, I want to tell you about something. We talked about grabbers and segues. This is a concurrent control for the Jay Shahbacher letter for Phillips, which is the biggest newsletter publisher in the world. And I want to read a little bit about a little bit of this letter to you because it illustrates perfectly a principle that needs to be illustrated. Dear friend, if you ever invest in the stock market, no matter how much or how little, this is going to be the most exciting letter you will ever read. Here is why in the next few months, a few insiders are going to get filthy rich while most other stock market investors are going to get the plug pulled on all their dreams. What's the reason for this? It's simple. You see looming on the horizon is a stock market disaster, which is going to be so severe subhead. It will make 1929 seem like a day at the beach to help you understand why this is going to happen. Why it has to happen. It is virtually inevitable. I need you to visualize something. Look, you know how much a million dollars is, right? However, like most people, you probably never actually seen a million dollars. Well, well, since a one inch stack of fresh new dollar bills amounts to $312 and it takes eight stacks to cover a square foot. What that means is if Chris knew one dollar bills came pouring out of your bathtub faucet, you could fill up your entire bathtub all the way to the soap dish with one million dollars. Now let's talk about a billion dollars. Since a billion is 1000 times a million, it would take an entire three bedroom house floor to ceiling attic basement and two car garage without a stick of furniture to hold your billion dollars. But the number I want you to visualize is 12 billion. $12 billion in neat little stacks of one dollar bills would fill the entire New York Stock Exchange after you took out all the computers, all the brokers, all the furniture and all the crazy traders screaming out by and sell. Now that was a grabber. Was it not? That got somebody's attention. The next thing we're going to talk about is the segue. Remember, I told you how important that is. Now we're going to segue into the segue. Why do I want you to visualize $12 billion? Because that's how much insider stock selling has been recorded in the last five and a half months. Insider selling, selling of company stock by corporate presidents, vice presidents and other key executives only happens for one reason and run reason only. It's this subhead. These insiders know their house of cards is about to come tumbling down. You may not know it because so many people want to keep it a secret, but there has been more, much more in Steiner's sock selling in the last one half year than ever before in history. Now, so far I've captured their attention. I read the headline to you yesterday, the amazing stock market secret of a financial genius who has made a profit every single year for 17 years in a row. That captures your attention. What I've just done is made you visualize something. You know, that's a way to do this without attention. In other words, write these words down. Picture this. If you're stuck or anything else, just say different picture this and then have them picture something. And so now I've captured their attention and I've got them. I've given them something interesting. And now I want to segue from this attention slash interesting copy to something else. Here's my segue. Okay. This dirty little secret is not being made public because if it were, it would cause the stock market equivalent of a run on the bank. Therefore, the powers that be have installed measures to keep the public in the dark for as long as possible. For example, October 11 1995 started out with the potential of being the day of the most devastating stock market plunge in history. Believe it or not, the Dow dropped 66 points subhead in just the first nine minutes of trading. Then the electronic plunge preventers automatically kicked in here and all computer program trading was immediately suspended. Yes, computer training trading did, of course, eventually resume, but only after the media experts had time to figure out the proper spend put on this event. So what you got on your nightly news was misinformation. But listen, these electronic safeguards only work on a temporary basis. They are not a cure. They are only cosmetic and the long run they have the same effect as putting a bandaid on a broken leg. But the long run is just about over. The real truth is now about to be revealed and it's not going to be pretty. Hey, from all this, it sounds like making a killing in the stock market has to be a very risky business, doesn't it? Well, that's just not true. You see, there is one man and only one man subhead who has made a profit every year for the last 17 years. How does he do it? I'm glad you asked. Listen, this guy has three main secrets and you're going to learn about all of them in this letter. Now, now I'm into the desire part of it, right? Because I've caught your attention. I've given you something interested and I've made you desire. And then, and I'm not going to read the entire letter, we go into a lot of the selling points and all the rest of it. And we take it right over, you know, to the point where I'm going to explain something to you that is that something I discovered years ago. And I want you to be aware about. The only reason that people buy from you in the sales letter or an ad can be summed up as the information that you put in your bullets, which are the little nuggets of copy or which should be in a bullet if they're not all the rest of it is just to get their attention, get their interest and prove it in the validator. And then here's what they're buying. And we told them. I told them. And just to get you to give it a try. I've decided to offer you the most valuable quote unquote bride in the world. All it is really is a few sheets of paper, 22 sheets to be exact. These 22 sheets of paper don't even come with a fancy cover. They're just simply stapled together. There's no fan. There's no color printing either. Just black typewriter income plain white paper. Strong words in plain English, but those words written by Jay Schabacher can change your life. Here's what they'll teach you. And then I enumerate him. Sheet number one, 85 mutual fund investments you need to get rid of right now. If you got any of these in your portfolio, you better sell right now. Sheet two, the four insider secrets, which allow you to instantly judge the real value of any fund. So it's impossible for you to be faked out. Sheet three, the number one rule you need in order to retire rich in the next 10 to 15 years, no matter where you are now financially, sheet four, how to legally snub Uncle Sam when he comes grubbing around for your retirement dollars. And how to pay yourself first every dollar you put aside. Sheet five, a handful of truly astonishing investments right under your nose, which will double every dollar you put in instantly. Sheet six, an insider special five point checklist to take all the fear and gray area out of your decisions to sell every time. This is your secret weapon in the fight to invest for really high returns. Sheet seven, the poverty trap, your IRA, on and on and on. By the time there's 22 of those people want them. I've got their attention. I've got their interest. And I didn't read it to you, but I did a lot to validate Jay Schaubacher in this letter. And so that you will know. This letter is done. It's pretty much of a perfect model letter. It's a headline. It's an attention getter. It's got good openings. It's got validation. It's got the yellow stuff highlighted. If there's anything that you wanted to model the shape Schaubacher letter would be very, very difficult to go wrong on. And the PS says if you subscribe for two years, you'll get an extra bribe. That's almost too good to believe you can read the details on the back of the order to find. I'm just making that up. Obviously I can't count them. I have read from some experts not to have over 70 characters and a wider white margin on each side to make it more readable. Can you comment on that? I used to kind of endorse that myself, but I'll tell you what I believe now. I believe that your letter should look crammed onto the page as though you could hardly get it into the paper to add a lot of excitement. I think there should be a lot of eye relief and it should look attractive, but that stuff about the margins being that many characters. I don't think that's good. I think probably that's the kind of stuff Herschel, Gordon Lewis or some other jerk would talk about that makes, you know, really very little difference. Don't worry about that. You know, if you want a foolproof way to do your sales, as you get my newsletter, make them look exactly like my newsletter. That's a foolproof way to do it. That's a way that works.
